Putting it all together, the patient had a recent COVID-19 infection, developed neurological symptoms, and the MRI and CSF show signs of an inflammatory brain process.

The patient's history of COVID-19 makes me think of post-viral complications.

Another consideration is viral encephalitis, but the MRI findings don't typically show the same cystic changes.
Additionally, the patient's recent COVID-19 infection could have contributed to a post-infectious immune response, but the imaging findings still lean more towards a parasitic infection.
Given his immigration history and the imaging, neurocysticercosis seems like the most likely diagnosis.
